note: Wheelchair accessible with a lift to the second tier and ramps; ground floor accessible and free for disabled visitors. The underground and upper tiers are NOT accessible. Pre-book timed entry.
Very wheelchair accessible and free entry; a metal ramp on the left of the portico gives step-free access. Piazza della Rotonda outside is flat with fairly uniform cobbles.
Accessible to reach but the viewing space is small and steps down toward the basin; extremely crowded, best at dawn. Manageable but tight for wheels/seniors in crowds.
Completely accessible Baroque square with the Bernini fountains; cobbled but the sidewalks are navigable, lots of cafes. Easy and pleasant.
Accessible routes throughout with lifts, free loaner manual wheelchairs, free disabled entry and skip-the-line; a few areas bar power chairs. Book an accessible route in advance.
Largely accessible with ramps and lifts and step-free entry (skip the line as a wheelchair user). The dome climb is not accessible. St Peter's Square is vast and flat.

Ancient site with uneven terrain, gravel, slopes and cobbles; only specific flat pathways are accessible and Palatine Hill needs an intrepid route or a special all-terrain chair. Hard for seniors/toddlers.
The famous steps themselves are stairs, but you can enjoy the view and Piazza di Spagna from the base without climbing. Surrounding area flat-ish with crowds.
Gallery has elevators to all floors (book timed tickets far ahead); Villa Borghese gardens are large, green and largely flat with a zoo - great for families. A relaxed accessible pairing.
An elevator takes wheelchair users to the top for the view, but much of the visit is a walk up and around the building and options are limited. Partly accessible.
Charming old neighbourhood; Piazza Santa Maria in Trastevere is fairly flat and easy, but many surrounding lanes are rough uneven cobbles. Great food/atmosphere.
